task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the mission specifications file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/dsn_sim_specs.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the spacecraft state and hardware in GMAT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing a Spacecraft object configured with SunMJ2000Eq Cartesian state, and an Antenna and Transponder added to its hardware list.

task_completion.description: Agent creates and configures the three required DSN ground stations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing GroundStation objects named DSS14, DSS43, and DSS63. Hardware tabs or resource trees should show a Transmitter, Receiver, and Antenna attached to each station.

task_completion.description: Agent sets up the deep-space propagator and simulation duration/step.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing a ForceModel including Sun, Earth, and Mars as point masses, and a Simulator/Propagator configured for a 7-day duration with a 3600-second step size.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the tracking file set to output Range and RangeRate measurements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing a TrackingFileSet object with Range and RangeRate selected as data types, and the output filename set to mars_tracking_sim.gmd.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the GMAT script and runs the simulation to generate the tracking data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing the script saved as dsn_sim.script in ~/GMAT_output/ and the mission running (e.g., 'Mission Run Completed' in the message window), OR a terminal window showing the existence/contents of ~/GMAT_output/mars_tracking_sim.gmd.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GMAT application to configure and run the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GMAT GUI (resource tree, configuration dialogs, mission sequence) or the agent writing a valid GMAT script (.script) in an editor, rather than just creating a fake text file named .gmd.

integrity.description: Verify the tracking data file was generated by the software, not manually typed or echo'd.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen using 'echo' or 'cat' to manually write a large block of fake tracking data directly into mars_tracking_sim.gmd.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually created and attached the required hardware components.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the GMAT GUI, the hardware components (Transponder, Antenna, Transmitter, Receiver) should be visibly created in the Resources tree and explicitly attached to the Spacecraft and GroundStations, not just mentioned in text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The mission specifications are located at /home/ga/Desktop/dsn_sim_specs.txt. The expected ground stations are DSS14, DSS43, and DSS63. The GMAT script must be saved to /home/ga/GMAT_output/dsn_sim.script. The simulated tracking data must be output to /home/ga/GMAT_output/mars_tracking_sim.gmd and contain at least 100 tracking records.
